examples.title: Epsilon Closure Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm to compute epsilon closure of a state in ε-NFA
examples.explanation: Uses DFS/BFS to find all states reachable via zero or more ε-transitions. Starts with the given state and explores all ε-transitions recursively.

examples.title: ε-NFA to NFA Conversion
examples.description: Convert ε-NFA to equivalent NFA by eliminating ε-transitions
examples.explanation: For each state and input symbol, computes ε-closure of states reachable via that input, eliminating epsilon transitions while preserving language.

examples.title: ε-NFA to DFA Subset Construction
examples.description: Convert ε-NFA to DFA using subset construction algorithm
examples.code:
function epsilonNFAtoDFA(enfa):
 dfa = new DFA()
 
 # Compute epsilon closures for all states
 allClosures = {}
 for each state q in enfa.states:
 allClosures[q] = epsilonClosure(q)
 
 # Start state is epsilon closure of enfa start state
 startSet = allClosures[enfa.startState]
 dfa.states = [startSet]
 dfa.startState = startSet
 
 queue = [startSet]
 
 while queue is not empty:
 currentSet = queue.pop()
 
 for each symbol a in enfa.alphabet:
 # Find all states reachable via symbol a
 reachable = SET()
 for each state s in currentSet:
 reachable.addAll(enfa.transition(s, a))
 
 # Apply epsilon closure to result
 newSet = SET()
 for each r in reachable:
 newSet.addAll(allClosures[r])
 
 # Add to DFA if new
 if newSet not in dfa.states:
 dfa.states.add(newSet)
 queue.push(newSet)
 
 dfa.transition[currentSet, a] = newSet
 
 # Mark final states (sets containing any enfa final state)
 dfa.finalStates = [s for s in dfa.states 
 if s intersects enfa.finalStates]
 
 return dfa
examples.explanation: Each DFA state represents a set of ε-NFA states. Uses epsilon closures to compute transitions, ensuring all ε-moves are properly handled.
